subject: Physics
solution:
The acceleration due to gravity at a depth $d$ below the earth's surface is given by:

$g_d = g\left(1 - \dfrac{d}{R}\right)$

The acceleration due to gravity at a height $h$ above the earth's surface (for $h \ll R$) is given by:

$g_h = g\left(1 - \dfrac{2h}{R}\right)$

Given $d = 16$ km and $h = 16$ km, we can find the values of $g_d$ and $g_h$:

$g_d = g\left(1 - \dfrac{16}{6400}\right) = g\left(1 - \dfrac{1}{400}\right)$

$g_h = g\left(1 - \dfrac{2 \times 16}{6400}\right) = g\left(1 - \dfrac{2}{400}\right)$

The magnitude of the change in acceleration due to gravity when moving from the depth $d$ to the height $h$ is:

$\Delta g = g_d - g_h = g\left(1 - \dfrac{1}{400}\right) - g\left(1 - \dfrac{2}{400}\right)$

$\Delta g = g\left(\dfrac{2}{400} - \dfrac{1}{400}\right) = \dfrac{g}{400}$

The percentage change in $g$ is approximately:

$\dfrac{\Delta g}{g} \times 100 = \dfrac{\dfrac{g}{400}}{g} \times 100 = \dfrac{100}{400} = 0.25\%$

Thus, the value of $\alpha$ is $0.25$.

Answer: $0.25$

Now since diameter is reduced to $1 / 3^{\text {rd }}$, radius also reduces to $1 / 3^{\text {rd }}$, keeping mass constant
New value of acceleration due to gravity on Earth's surface is
$\mathrm{g}^{\prime}=\frac{\mathrm{GM}}{\left(\frac{\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{e}}}{3}\right)^2}=9 \frac{\mathrm{GMe}}{\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{e}}^2}=9 \mathrm{~g}$

subject: Physics
solution:
Newton's law of gravitation state that gravitational force between two point masses is directly proportional to the product of their masses m1 and m2 and inversely proportional to the square of the distance r between them. It is stated as F=Gm1m2r2

A planet moves around the sun in a circular path for which the gravitational force of attraction on the planet by the sun provides the necessary centripetal force. This centripetal force is always directed towards the centre of the sun at each point of its path which is responsible for circular motion of planet.

From Kepler's Third Law, we know that the squares of the orbital periods of the planets are directly proportional to the cubes of the semi-major axes of their orbits.

⇒T2∝a3

subject: Physics
solution:
Volume of removed sphere
$$
V_{\text {remo }}=\frac{4}{3} \pi\left(\frac{R}{2}\right)^3=\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3\left(\frac{1}{8}\right)
$$
Volume of the sphere (remaining)
$$
\begin{aligned}
&V_{\text {remain }}=\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3-\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3\left(\frac{1}{8}\right) \\
&=\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3\left(\frac{7}{8}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
Therefore mass of sphere carved and remaining sphere are at respectively $\frac{1}{8} \mathrm{M}$ and $\frac{7}{8} \mathrm{M}$

Therefore, gravitational force between these two sphere,
$$
F=\frac{G \mathrm{M} m}{r^2}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
&=\frac{G \frac{7 M}{8} \times \frac{1}{8} M}{(3 R)^2}=\frac{7}{64 \times 9} \frac{G M^2}{R^2} \\
&\simeq \frac{41}{3600} \frac{\mathrm{GM}^2}{\mathrm{R}^2}
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Physics
solution:
$g^{\prime}=\frac{G M}{(R+h)^2}$, acceleration due to gravity at height $h$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \frac{g}{9}=\frac{G M}{R^2} \cdot \frac{R^2}{(R+h)^2}=g\left(\frac{R}{R+h}\right)^2 \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{1}{9}=\left(\frac{R}{R+h}\right)^2 \Rightarrow \frac{R}{R+h}=\frac{1}{3} \\
& \Rightarrow 3 R=R+h \Rightarrow 2 R=h
\end{aligned}
$$

& \therefore \mathrm{v}_0=\left(\frac{g \mathrm{R}^2}{\mathrm{R}+\mathrm{X}}\right)^{1 / 2} \\

$$

subject: Physics
options: Time taken by them to reach the earth's surface will be independent of the properties of their materials
Insulating ball will reach the earth's surface earlier than the metal ball
Both will reach the earth's surface simultaneously
Metal ball will reach the earth's surface earlier than the insulating ball
solution: When a spherical insulating ball and a spherical metallic ball of same size and mass are dropped from the same height, then magnetic field will act on metallic ball. If the earth's magnetic field is taken into account, then an induced eddy current will form inside the spherical metallic ball, thus retarding its motion. Hence, it will take more time than insulating ball.

subject: Physics
solution:
Gravitational potential energy on the earth surface Ui=-GMmR

Gravitational potential energy at a height h above the earth's surface Uf=-GMmR+h=-GMmR+3R=-GMm4R

Gain in gravitational potential energy  

ΔU=Uf-Ui=-GMm4R+GMmR=GMm+4GMm4R= 3GMm4R

Gravitational potential energy in terms of acceleration due to gravity is ∆U=34mgR

=34×1×10×64×105

=48 MJ

subject: Physics
solution:
Escape velocity $v_e = R\sqrt{\frac{8\pi G\rho}{3}} \propto R\sqrt{\rho}$.
Planet B: $\rho_B = 0.1\rho_A$, $R_B = 0.1R_A$.
$\frac{v_B}{v_A} = \frac{0.1R_A\sqrt{0.1\rho_A}}{R_A\sqrt{\rho_A}} = 0.1\sqrt{0.1} = \frac{1}{10\sqrt{10}}$.
$v_B = 10 \times \frac{1}{10\sqrt{10}} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{10}}$ km/s $= \frac{1000}{\sqrt{10}} = 100\sqrt{10}$ m/s.
